class SandboxesController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_sandbox, only: [ :show, :update, :destroy, :start, :stop, :retry, :logs, :metrics ]
  before_action :set_archived_sandbox, only: [ :archive_restore ]

  def new
    authorize Sandbox
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    @btrfs_available = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    @defaults = Setting.instance
  end

  def show
    @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
                           .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    @routes = @sandbox.routes.order(:created_at)
    @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
  end

  def update
    if @sandbox.update(params.require(:sandbox).permit(:name))
      redirect_to @sandbox, notice: "Sandbox renamed to #{@sandbox.name}."
    else
      @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
                             .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
      @routes = @sandbox.routes.order(:created_at)
      @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      flash.now[:alert] = @sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(", ")
      render :show, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def create
    authorize Sandbox

    from_snapshot_name = params[:snapshot].presence

    image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
      snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: Current.user, name: from_snapshot_name)
      snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{Current.user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    else
      params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    end

    # Build sandbox record
    # Note: temporary sandboxes can only be created via CLI
    defaults = Setting.instance
    sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.build(
      name: params.require(:name),
      status: "pending",
      image: image,
      persistent_volume: params[:persistent] == "1",
      mount_home: params[:mount_home] == "1",
      data_path: params[:data_path].presence,
      tailscale: params[:tailscale] == "1",
      vnc_enabled: params[:vnc_enabled] != "0",
      vnc_geometry: Sandbox::VNC_GEOMETRIES.include?(params[:vnc_geometry]) ? params[:vnc_geometry] : "1280x900",
      vnc_depth: Sandbox::VNC_DEPTHS.include?(params[:vnc_depth].to_i) ? params[:vnc_depth].to_i : 24,
      docker_enabled: params[:docker_enabled] != "0",
      temporary: false
    )

    if sandbox.persistent_volume
      sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    end

    if sandbox.save
      # Enqueue async job
      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)

      # Optimistic redirect - dashboard will update via Turbo
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Creating sandcastle #{sandbox.name}..."
    else
      @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
      flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  rescue => e
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{e.message}"
    render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
  end

  def metrics
    points = @sandbox.container_metrics.recent
      .map { |m| { t: m.recorded_at.to_i, cpu: m.cpu_percent, mem: m.memory_mb.round(0) } }

    # Append current live stats so graphs render instantly on page load
    if @sandbox.status == "running" && @sandbox.container_id.present?
      full = Rails.cache.fetch("sandbox_stats_full:#{@sandbox.id}", expires_in: 4.seconds) do
        raw = Docker::Container.get(@sandbox.container_id).stats(stream: false)
        next nil if raw.blank?
        {
          cpu_percent: StatsCalculator.cpu_percent(raw),
          memory_mb: StatsCalculator.memory_mb(raw),
          memory_limit_mb: (raw.dig("memory_stats", "limit") || 0) / 1_048_576.0,
          net_rx: 0, net_tx: 0, disk_read: 0, disk_write: 0, pids: 0
        }
      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
        nil
      end
      points << { t: Time.current.to_i, cpu: full[:cpu_percent], mem: full[:memory_mb].round(0) } if full
    end

    render json: points
  end

  def logs
    tail = (params[:tail] || 200).to_i.clamp(1, 5000)
    @tail = tail
    @logs = SandboxManager.new.logs(sandbox: @sandbox, tail: tail, timestamps: true)
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    @logs = nil
    @log_error = e.message
  end

  def destroy
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    user = Current.user
    archive = user.effective_archive_retention_days > 0

    @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
    SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id, archive: archive)

    notice = archive ? "Archiving sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." : "Destroying sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..."
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: notice }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def archive_restore
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("restoring")
    SandboxRestoreJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Restoring sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..."
  end

  def start
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("starting")
    SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Starting sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def stop
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("stopping")
    SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Stopping sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def retry
    return unless @sandbox.job_failed?

    @sandbox.update!(job_error: nil)

    case @sandbox.status
    when "destroyed", "pending"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Cannot retry creation. Please create a new sandbox."
    when "stopped"
      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Retrying start..."
    when "running"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Sandbox is already running"
    end
  end

  private

  def set_sandbox
    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize @sandbox
  end

  def set_archived_sandbox
    @sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.archived.find(params[:id])
    authorize @sandbox, :archive_restore?
  end
end

class SandboxesController < ApplicationController
  before_action :set_sandbox, only: [ :show, :update, :destroy, :start, :stop, :retry, :logs, :metrics ]
  before_action :set_archived_sandbox, only: [ :archive_restore, :purge ]

  def new
    authorize Sandbox
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    @btrfs_available = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
    @defaults = Setting.instance
  end

  def show
    @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
                           .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
    @routes = @sandbox.routes.order(:created_at)
    @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
  end

  def update
    if @sandbox.update(params.require(:sandbox).permit(:name))
      redirect_to @sandbox, notice: "Sandbox renamed to #{@sandbox.name}."
    else
      @sandbox_snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
                             .select { |s| s[:source_sandbox] == @sandbox.name }
      @routes = @sandbox.routes.order(:created_at)
      @btrfs = BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      flash.now[:alert] = @sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(", ")
      render :show, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def create
    authorize Sandbox

    from_snapshot_name = params[:snapshot].presence

    image = if from_snapshot_name.present?
      snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: Current.user, name: from_snapshot_name)
      snap&.docker_image || "sc-snap-#{Current.user.name}:#{from_snapshot_name}"
    else
      params[:image].presence || SandboxManager::DEFAULT_IMAGE
    end

    # Build sandbox record
    # Note: temporary sandboxes can only be created via CLI
    defaults = Setting.instance
    sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.build(
      name: params.require(:name),
      status: "pending",
      image: image,
      persistent_volume: params[:persistent] == "1",
      mount_home: params[:mount_home] == "1",
      data_path: params[:data_path].presence,
      tailscale: params[:tailscale] == "1",
      vnc_enabled: params[:vnc_enabled] != "0",
      vnc_geometry: Sandbox::VNC_GEOMETRIES.include?(params[:vnc_geometry]) ? params[:vnc_geometry] : "1280x900",
      vnc_depth: Sandbox::VNC_DEPTHS.include?(params[:vnc_depth].to_i) ? params[:vnc_depth].to_i : 24,
      docker_enabled: params[:docker_enabled] != "0",
      temporary: false
    )

    if sandbox.persistent_volume
      sandbox.volume_path = "#{SandboxManager::DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    end

    if sandbox.save
      # Enqueue async job
      SandboxProvisionJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: sandbox.id)

      # Optimistic redirect - dashboard will update via Turbo
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Creating sandcastle #{sandbox.name}..."
    else
      @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
      flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{sandbox.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
      render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  rescue => e
    @snapshots = SandboxManager.new.list_snapshots(user: Current.user)
    flash.now[:alert] = "Failed to create sandbox: #{e.message}"
    render :new, status: :unprocessable_entity
  end

  def metrics
    points = @sandbox.container_metrics.recent
      .map { |m| { t: m.recorded_at.to_i, cpu: m.cpu_percent, mem: m.memory_mb.round(0) } }

    # Append current live stats so graphs render instantly on page load
    if @sandbox.status == "running" && @sandbox.container_id.present?
      full = Rails.cache.fetch("sandbox_stats_full:#{@sandbox.id}", expires_in: 4.seconds) do
        raw = Docker::Container.get(@sandbox.container_id).stats(stream: false)
        next nil if raw.blank?
        {
          cpu_percent: StatsCalculator.cpu_percent(raw),
          memory_mb: StatsCalculator.memory_mb(raw),
          memory_limit_mb: (raw.dig("memory_stats", "limit") || 0) / 1_048_576.0,
          net_rx: 0, net_tx: 0, disk_read: 0, disk_write: 0, pids: 0
        }
      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
        nil
      end
      points << { t: Time.current.to_i, cpu: full[:cpu_percent], mem: full[:memory_mb].round(0) } if full
    end

    render json: points
  end

  def logs
    tail = (params[:tail] || 200).to_i.clamp(1, 5000)
    @tail = tail
    @logs = SandboxManager.new.logs(sandbox: @sandbox, tail: tail, timestamps: true)
  rescue SandboxManager::Error => e
    @logs = nil
    @log_error = e.message
  end

  def destroy
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    user = Current.user
    archive = user.effective_archive_retention_days > 0

    @sandbox.start_job("destroying")
    SandboxDestroyJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id, archive: archive)

    notice = archive ? "Archiving sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." : "Destroying sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..."
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: notice }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def archive_restore
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("restoring")
    SandboxRestoreJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Restoring sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..."
  end

  def start
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("starting")
    SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Starting sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def stop
    if @sandbox.job_in_progress?
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Operation already in progress"
      return
    end

    @sandbox.start_job("stopping")
    SandboxStopJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { redirect_to root_path, notice: "Stopping sandcastle #{@sandbox.name}..." }
      format.turbo_stream { render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(@sandbox, partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: @sandbox }) }
    end
  end

  def retry
    return unless @sandbox.job_failed?

    @sandbox.update!(job_error: nil)

    case @sandbox.status
    when "destroyed", "pending"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Cannot retry creation. Please create a new sandbox."
    when "stopped"
      SandboxStartJob.perform_later(sandbox_id: @sandbox.id)
      redirect_to root_path, notice: "Retrying start..."
    when "running"
      redirect_to root_path, alert: "Sandbox is already running"
    end
  end

  private

  def set_sandbox
    @sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize @sandbox
  end

  def set_archived_sandbox
    @sandbox = Current.user.sandboxes.archived.find(params[:id])
    authorize @sandbox, :archive_restore?
  end
end

end

  # Restore an archived sandbox: recreate the container from the existing volume.
  # The container is started immediately; status is set to "running".
  def restore_from_archive(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not archived" unless sandbox.status == "archived"

    user = sandbox.user

    ensure_image(sandbox.image)
    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)

    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    sandbox.update!(archived_at: nil)

    if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to restore archived sandbox: #{e.message}"
  end

  def start(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"

    user = sandbox.user

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        old.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # already gone
      end
    end

    # Reset bind-mount directory permissions before starting the new container.
    # After a previous Sysbox run, dirs may be owned by a remapped UID that
    # Rails can't chmod — ensure_mount_dirs uses sudo to reset to 777.
    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)

    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)

    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
  end

  def stop(sandbox:)
    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"

    begin

class SandboxManager
  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
  DEFAULT_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest"
  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"

  class Error < StandardError; end

  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, persistent: false, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
      name: name,
      image: image,
      status: "pending",
      persistent_volume: persistent,
      mount_home: mount_home,
      data_path: data_path,
      temporary: temporary
    )

    if persistent
      sandbox.volume_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    end

    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    sandbox.validate!

    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)

    # Now safe to save
    sandbox.save!

    # Pull image
    ensure_image(image)

    # Create and start container
    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)

    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
  rescue => e
    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    raise Error, e.message
  end

  # Public method for job usage
  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    container = Docker::Container.create(
      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
      "Image" => sandbox.image,
      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
      "HostConfig" => {
        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
      },
      "NetworkingConfig" => {
        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
      }
    )

    container.start
    container.refresh!
    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
      container.stop rescue nil
      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
      raise Error, "Container failed to start: #{state_error}"
    end
    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")

    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
  end

  # Public method for job usage
  def ensure_image(image)
    Docker::Image.get(image)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Public method for job usage
  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)

    # Ensure user base directory is writable (may be root-owned from previous install)
    ensure_dir("#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}")

    if sandbox.mount_home
      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
      ensure_dir(dir)
      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
    end
    if sandbox.data_path.present?
      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)

      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
      ensure_dir(dir)
      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
    end
    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
      ensure_dir(sandbox.volume_path)
      prepare_bind_mount(sandbox.volume_path)
    end
    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
      ensure_dir(dir)
      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
    end
  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
  end

  def destroy(sandbox:, keep_volume: false, archive: false)
    begin
      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort terminal cleanup
    end

    begin
      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort VNC cleanup
    end

    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?

    if sandbox.tailscale?
      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        container.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Container already gone
      end
    end

    if archive
      # Soft-delete: keep volume on disk, mark as archived.
      # Rename to free the original name for reuse.
      archived_name = "#{Time.current.strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}-#{sandbox.name}"
      sandbox.update!(status: "archived", container_id: nil, archived_at: Time.current, name: archived_name)
    else
      unless keep_volume
        FileUtils.rm_rf(sandbox.volume_path) if sandbox.volume_path.present?
      end
      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
    end
  end

  # Restore an archived sandbox: recreate the container from the existing volume.
  # The container is started immediately; status is set to "running".
  def restore_from_archive(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is not archived" unless sandbox.status == "archived"

    user = sandbox.user

    ensure_image(sandbox.image)
    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)

    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    sandbox.update!(archived_at: nil)

    if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to restore archived sandbox: #{e.message}"
  end

  def start(sandbox:)
    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"

    user = sandbox.user

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        old.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # already gone
      end
    end

    # Reset bind-mount directory permissions before starting the new container.
    # After a previous Sysbox run, dirs may be owned by a remapped UID that
    # Rails can't chmod — ensure_mount_dirs uses sudo to reset to 777.
    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)

    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)

    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
  end

  def stop(sandbox:)
    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"

    begin
      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort terminal cleanup
    end

    begin
      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort VNC cleanup
    end

    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        container.stop(t: 10)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # already gone
      end
    end

    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
    sandbox
  end

  SERVICES = %w[docker vnc].freeze

  def service_start(sandbox:, service:)
    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    user = sandbox.user.name

    case service
    when "docker"
      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x dockerd > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; dockerd --storage-driver=overlay2 --mtu=$(ip link show eth0 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'mtu \\K[0-9]+' || echo 1500) &>/var/log/dockerd.log & echo started" ])
    when "vnc"
      geometry = sandbox.vnc_geometry || "1280x900"
      depth = sandbox.vnc_depth || 24
      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x Xvnc > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry #{geometry} -depth #{depth} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &' && su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &' && websockify -addr :6080 -target localhost:5900 -url /websockify &>/var/log/websockify.log & echo started" ])
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Container not found"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to start #{service}: #{e.message}"
  end

  def service_stop(sandbox:, service:)
    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)

    case service
    when "docker"
      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x dockerd 2>/dev/null; pkill -x containerd 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
    when "vnc"
      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x websockify 2>/dev/null; pkill -x openbox 2>/dev/null; pkill -x Xvnc 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Container not found"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to stop #{service}: #{e.message}"
  end

  def logs(sandbox:, tail: 200, timestamps: false)
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, follow: false, tail: tail, timestamps: timestamps)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    raise Error, "Container not found"
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to fetch logs: #{e.message}"
  end

  def status(sandbox:)
    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
    info = container.json
    {
      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
    }
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    { state: "not_found" }
  end

  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
  #
  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data", "workspace"
  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?

    user = sandbox.user
    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data workspace]

    snap = Snapshot.new(
      user: user,
      name: name,
      label: label,
      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
      data_subdir: data_subdir
    )

    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("container")
      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
      image = container.commit(
        repo: repo,
        tag: name,
        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
      )
      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
    end

    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
      end
    end

    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      if data_subdir.present?
        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
      else
        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
      end
      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
      end
    end

    # ── Workspace layer (BTRFS only) ─────────────────────────────────────────
    if requested_layers.include?("workspace") && sandbox.persistent_volume? && sandbox.volume_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
      workspace_src  = sandbox.volume_path
      workspace_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/workspace"
      if Dir.exist?(workspace_src)
        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(workspace_src, workspace_dest)
        # Store workspace alongside data_snapshot when no data layer taken
        snap.data_snapshot ||= workspace_dest
        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(workspace_dest)
      end
    end

    snap.save!
    snap
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
    {
      name: snap.name,
      image: snap.docker_image,
      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
      created_at: snap.created_at
    }
  end

  def list_snapshots(user:)
    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
  end

  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
  end

  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)

    if snap
      # Remove Docker image
      if snap.docker_image.present?
        begin
          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
          # Already gone
        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
        end
      end

      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
        begin
          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
        end
      end

      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
        begin
          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
        end
      end

      snap.destroy!
    else
      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
      begin
        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
      end
    end
  end

  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
    user = sandbox.user
    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?

    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)

    # Determine the Docker image to use
    if snap&.docker_image.present?
      image_ref = snap.docker_image
    else
      # Fall back to legacy naming
      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
    end

    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))

    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
    if restore_container
      begin
        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
      end
    end

    begin
      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort terminal cleanup
    end

    begin
      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
      # best-effort VNC cleanup
    end

    if sandbox.tailscale?
      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    if sandbox.container_id.present?
      begin
        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
        old_container.delete(force: true)
      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
        # Already gone
      end
    end

    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if restore_home
      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
      begin
        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
      end
    end

    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    if restore_data
      if snap.data_subdir.present?
        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
      else
        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
      end
      begin
        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
      end
    end

    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image

    container = Docker::Container.create(
      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
      "Image" => final_image,
      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
      "HostConfig" => {
        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
      },
      "NetworkingConfig" => {
        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
      }
    )

    container.start
    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, image: final_image, status: "running")

    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately after restore.
    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?

    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    end

    sandbox
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
  end

  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"

    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
      repo_tags.each do |tag|
        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
        next unless repo == repo_prefix
        next if tag_name.blank?
        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)

        Snapshot.create!(
          user: user,
          name: tag_name,
          docker_image: tag,
          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
        )
      end
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
  end

  def snapshot_json(snap)
    {
      name: snap.name,
      label: snap.label,
      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
      layers: snap.layers,
      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
      home_size: snap.home_size,
      data_size: snap.data_size,
      total_size: snap.total_size,
      created_at: snap.created_at
    }
  end

  def connect_info(sandbox:)
    user = sandbox.user.name

    unless sandbox.tailscale?
      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
    end

    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available — is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?

    {
      host: ts_ip,
      port: 22,
      user: user,
      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
    }
  end

  private

  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
    max_attempts.times do
      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status

      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
      if sandbox.status != "running"
        sleep delay
        next
      end

      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?

      sleep delay
    end
    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
    nil
  end

  def connect_to_network(container)
    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
    network.connect(container.id)
  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
  end

  def container_env(user, sandbox)
    env = [
      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
    ]
    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
    env << "GITHUB_USERNAME=#{user.github_username}" if user.github_username.present?
    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
    env << "SANDCASTLE_DOCKER_ENABLED=#{sandbox.docker_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
    env
  end

  def container_runtime
    @container_runtime ||= begin
      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
        "sysbox-runc"
      else
        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
        "runc"
      end
    end
  end

  # mkdir_p with self-healing: if EACCES, fix the parent dir's ownership
  # via a short-lived Docker container (only way when running as non-root
  # inside a container with Docker socket access).
  def ensure_dir(path)
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
  rescue Errno::EACCES
    docker_chown(File.dirname(path))
    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
  end

  # Ensure a bind-mounted directory is world-writable so the sandbox user
  # (non-root inside the Sysbox container) can write to it.
  def prepare_bind_mount(path)
    stat = File.stat(path)
    return if stat.mode & 0o777 == 0o777
    return if system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "777", path)
    docker_chmod(path, "777")
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    # directory disappeared — race condition, ignore
  end

  # Fix ownership and permissions of a host path via a busybox container.
  def docker_chown(path)
    docker_run_fix(path, "sh", "-c", "chown #{Process.uid}:#{Process.gid} /mnt && chmod 755 /mnt")
  end

  def docker_chmod(path, mode)
    docker_run_fix(path, "chmod", mode, "/mnt")
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("docker_chmod(#{path}) failed: #{e.message}")
  end

  def docker_run_fix(host_path, *cmd)
    image = fix_image
    c = Docker::Container.create(
      "Image" => image, "Cmd" => cmd,
      "HostConfig" => { "Binds" => [ "#{host_path}:/mnt" ] }
    )
    c.start
    result = c.wait(30)
    exit_code = result&.dig("StatusCode") || -1
    unless exit_code == 0
      raise Error, "docker_run_fix failed (exit #{exit_code}) for #{host_path}: #{cmd.join(' ')}"
    end
  ensure
    c&.delete(force: true) rescue nil
  end

  # Pick an image guaranteed to be on this Docker daemon.
  # Prefer busybox (tiny), fall back to alpine, then any local image.
  def fix_image
    %w[busybox:latest alpine:latest].each do |img|
      begin
        return img if Docker::Image.get(img)
      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
        next
      end
    end
    # Last resort: use any image already present
    all = Docker::Image.all
    raise Error, "No local images available for docker_run_fix" if all.empty?
    tags = all.first.info["RepoTags"]
    tags&.first || all.first.id
  end

  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
    binds = []
    if sandbox.mount_home
      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
    end
    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
      binds << "#{sandbox.volume_path}:/workspace"
    end
    if sandbox.data_path.present?
      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
      binds << "#{host_path}:/data"
    end
    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
    end
    binds
  end
end

content.text:
Released as **v0.8.56**. Summary of changes:

1. **Archive rename**: When a sandbox is archived, it's renamed to `<YYYYMMDDHHMMSS>-<name>` (e.g. `20260307143022-sysbox`), freeing the original name for reuse. On restore, the timestamp prefix is stripped back.

2. **"Really delete" button**: Archived sandbox cards now show a red "Really delete" button with a confirmation dialog. This permanently destroys the sandbox and its volume data. Available to the sandbox owner (changed from admin-only).
Already done — everything was committed and pushed as part of the release. The latest commit on `main` is `6244b31` (v0.8.56).

So when SSH connects, it runs the tmux command in a non-login shell context, which means `/etc/profile.d/ssh-agent-switcher.sh` never gets sourced. The tmux session then spawns a login shell that does source the script, but by that point the SSH_AUTH_SOCK from the forwarded agent socket isn't available in the tmux environment anymore—it was only set in the initial SSH session context and doesn't carry over to the new shell.

This is the classic tmux and SSH agent forwarding issue. When you SSH in with `-A`, the agent socket gets set as an environment variable, but tmux either creates a completely new shell that doesn't inherit it, or if the session already exists, it's using the old environment from whenever that session was first created. The forwarded socket from the current SSH connection never makes it into the tmux shell's environment.

The ssh-agent-switcher script is designed to solve exactly this—it creates a stable socket path that persists across sessions and proxies connections to whatever forwarded agent is currently available. The real challenge is that the daemon needs to detect and use the new forwarded agent socket from the current SSH session rather than sticking with an old one. The SSH session's environment variables aren't necessarily visible to the tmux shell, which is the core problem. Can't reach sandman from here. Let me debug by analyzing the code flow instead.

The core issue: when `sc c sysbox` runs, SSH executes `tmux new-session -A -s main` as a **non-login shell command**. `/etc/profile.d/ssh-agent-switcher.sh` is only sourced by **login shells**. So on the initial SSH connection:

1. SSH connects with `-A` → sshd sets `SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/tmp/ssh-XXXX/agent.PID`
2. SSH runs `tmux new-session -A -s main` (non-login shell — profile.d NOT sourced)
3. tmux starts a login shell in the pane → profile.d runs, daemon starts
4. But on **reattach** (`-A` flag to tmux), no new shell starts, so the daemon never sees the NEW forwarded agent socket

The problem: `ssh-agent-switcher --daemon` needs to be invoked **before tmux** in the SSH session context, where `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` still points to the forwarded agent. Let me fix the remote command.

content.text:
Released as **v0.8.57**.

**Root cause**: The remote command was just `tmux new-session -A -s main`, which runs in a non-login shell. `ssh-agent-switcher` was only started via `/etc/profile.d/` inside tmux's login shell — too late, because on tmux reattach no new shell starts, and even on first connect the forwarded agent socket (`SSH_AUTH_SOCK` from sshd) was already overridden.

**Fix**: The remote command now runs `ssh-agent-switcher --daemon` **before** tmux, in the SSH session context where `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` still points to the real forwarded agent socket:

```
ssh-agent-switcher --daemon; export SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/tmp/ssh-agent.$USER; tmux new-session -A -s main
```

This ensures the daemon captures the forwarded agent on every connection, including reattach.
Already done — committed as `c81b897` and pushed to `main`, released as v0.8.57.

The user is showing two issues:
1. First connection via mosh exits immediately (probably the bash -c fix broke something)
2. Second connection shows `ssh-add -l` returns "communication with agent failed" - meaning ssh-agent-switcher daemon isn't working properly

Let me think about this more carefully.

For issue 1: mosh might be exiting because `bash -c` with the compound command has quoting issues. Let me check how the args are passed.

For issue 2: The user connected and `ssh-add -l` shows the agent socket exists but communication fails. This means:
- `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is set (to `/tmp/ssh-agent.$USER`)


- But the daemon listening on that socket isn't actually forwarding requests to the real agent that sshd set up

The real issue is that when SSH connects with `-A`, it creates a forwarded agent socket in a temporary location, but my daemon needs to know where that socket is and proxy requests to it—and I'm not sure the daemon is actually capturing or using that forwarded socket path correctly.

Looking at how ssh-agent-switcher actually works, the daemon starts listening on a fixed socket and proxies connections to whatever agent was active when it launched. So if the daemon started before the SSH connection was established, it wouldn't know about the forwarded agent socket at all. The daemon might need the forwarded socket path passed to it explicitly, or it needs to be restarted after the SSH connection is made. the real issue is that the forwarded socket path changes on each SSH reconnection, so the daemon loses its connection. The classic tmux solution is to create a stable symlink that points to whatever the current forwarded socket is, then always reference that symlink instead—this way when you reconnect and get a new socket, you just update the symlink and everything keeps working.

But there's a catch: mosh doesn't support SSH agent forwarding at all, so the forwarded socket won't persist across mosh connections in the first place.

content.text:
The problem is twofold:
1. Mosh joins args with spaces, losing quoting — `bash -c "if ..."` becomes `bash -c if command ...`
2. Even when it works, `ssh-agent-switcher --daemon` on re-run doesn't pick up the new forwarded socket

The cleanest fix: add a wrapper script to the sandbox image that handles all of this, then use that simple executable as the remote command.

file.content:
# Thin layer on top of the pre-built base image.
# Changes here (entrypoint, config, scripts) build in seconds.
# To rebuild the base: trigger the "Rebuild Sandbox Base Image" workflow
# or change files in images/sandbox/base/.
FROM ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox-base:latest

ARG SANDCASTLE_VERSION=dev

# Always pull latest security patches and package updates
RUN apt-get update && apt-get upgrade -y && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Bake version into the image
RUN echo "${SANDCASTLE_VERSION}" > /etc/sandcastle-version

# ssh-agent-switcher — multiplexes SSH agent sockets per-identity
ARG TARGETARCH
RUN SAS_VERSION="v1.0.1.1-tc" \
    && SAS_ARCH=$([ "$TARGETARCH" = "arm64" ] && echo "aarch64" || echo "x86_64") \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/thieso2/ssh-agent-switcher/releases/download/${SAS_VERSION}/ssh-agent-switcher-${SAS_ARCH}-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" \
       | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin ssh-agent-switcher \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/ssh-agent-switcher \
    && printf '/usr/local/bin/ssh-agent-switcher --daemon 2>/dev/null || true\nexport SSH_AUTH_SOCK="/tmp/ssh-agent.${USER}"\n' \
       > /etc/profile.d/ssh-agent-switcher.sh

COPY entrypoint.sh /entrypoint.sh
COPY docker-restart.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-restart
RUN chmod +x /entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/docker-restart

COPY startchrome.sh /usr/local/bin/google-chrome
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/google-chrome \
    && ln -sf /usr/local/bin/google-chrome /usr/local/bin/startchrome

COPY tmux.conf /etc/tmux.conf
COPY gitconfig /etc/gitconfig

LABEL org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/thieso2/sandcastle"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="Sandcastle sandbox image — Ubuntu 25.10 with Docker-in-Docker, SSH, and dev tools"
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT"

WORKDIR /workspace
EXPOSE 22 5900 6080 7681 7682
EXPOSE 60000-61000/udp

ENTRYPOINT ["/entrypoint.sh"]

content.text: The cleanest approach: a wrapper script in the sandbox image that handles agent forwarding with a simple symlink (proven, no daemon dependency), then execs tmux. This avoids all quoting/shell-builtin issues with mosh.

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.
#
# When SSH connects with -A, sshd creates a temporary agent socket.
# We symlink a stable path to it so tmux sessions always find the
# latest forwarded agent — even on reattach.

if [ -n "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ] && [ -S "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ]; then
    mkdir -p ~/.ssh 2>/dev/null
    ln -sf "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ~/.ssh/agent_sock
    export SSH_AUTH_SOCK=~/.ssh/agent_sock
fi

exec tmux new-session -A -s main

Released as **v0.8.59**. Two things need to happen:

1. **CLI update** (`brew upgrade sandcastle`) — remote command is now just `sc-tmux`
2. **Sandbox image rebuild** — to get `/usr/local/bin/sc-tmux` into the image

The `sc-tmux` script:
- Symlinks `~/.ssh/agent_sock` → current forwarded agent socket (`$SSH_AUTH_SOCK` from sshd)
- Sets `SSH_AUTH_SOCK=~/.ssh/agent_sock` (stable path that survives tmux reattach)
- Execs `tmux new-session -A -s main`

No more shell builtins in the remote command, so mosh `execvp` works. No dependency on `ssh-agent-switcher` daemon functioning correctly.

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue clearly!

1. The ssh-agent-switcher daemon at `/tmp/ssh-agent.thies` actually WORKS - it responds to `ssh-add -l` and shows the keys.
2. The `sc-tmux` script is there and correct.
3. The tmux environment has `REDACTED.1123` - this is an OLD forwarded agent socket from a previous SSH connection that no longer exists!

So the problem is:
- `sc-tmux` runs and sees `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` pointing to the forwarded socket


- It creates a symlink `~/.ssh/agent_sock` → forwarded socket
- It starts/attaches tmux with `SSH_AUTH_SOCK=~/.ssh/agent_sock`
- But the tmux session's environment still has the old `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` from when it was first created, and tmux's `update-environment` feature isn't updating it on attach

The tmux env shows `/tmp/ssh-s5GaFsB6L0/agent.1123` instead of `~/.ssh/agent_sock`, which means either `sc-tmux` didn't run properly or the session was created before the script existed.

When `sc-tmux` execs `tmux new-session -A -s main` with the symlink set, the `-A` flag should attach to an existing session and trigger environment updates if `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is in `update-environment`. But `tmux show-environment` shows the old socket, suggesting either this wasn't the entry point for the original session, or `update-environment` doesn't actually refresh the session-level environment that `show-environment` displays.

The real issue is that while tmux updates its session environment when a client attaches, the shell running inside the pane doesn't get that update—it keeps whatever `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` value it inherited when the pane was first created, which came from the profile.d script setting it to the daemon socket path. So the daemon socket works when I explicitly set it, but inside tmux the `ssh-add` command fails—which means `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is pointing somewhere else entirely, probably either the dead symlink at `~/.ssh/agent_sock` or an old forwarded socket that tmux cached. I'm guessing the tmux config is overriding the environment variable on session creation.

The real issue is that when `sc-tmux` attaches, it sets `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` to the symlink, tmux updates the session environment, and new panes inherit that broken value. The fix is to make profile.d point to the symlink instead of the daemon directly, so `sc-tmux` can update the symlink target and everything just works.

But wait—profile.d only runs once when the shell starts, so if the symlink was stale then, it stays stale. The key insight is that `sc-tmux` updates the symlink on every connection before tmux attaches, so if the shell's `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` points to the symlink path itself rather than the daemon path, it will always resolve to the current forwarded socket. The problem is the shell has `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` hardcoded to `/tmp/ssh-agent.thies` from when profile.d ran initially.

The fix is to change profile.d to export the symlink path instead, and let `sc-tmux` keep that symlink fresh on each connection. But the daemon at `/tmp/ssh-agent.thies` actually works too, so I need to understand why the user is getting "communication with agent failed" inside their tmux session—let me check what `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is actually set to in there.

I'm wondering if the daemon becomes unresponsive or hits a permission issue over time, or if something about the existing tmux session state is different from my fresh test.

content.text:
Look at that! The first `ssh-add -l` failed, but then subsequent calls **succeed**. The daemon at `/tmp/ssh-agent.thies` works, but it's **lazy** — it needs an active forwarded agent connection to wake up and capture the keys. The first SSH connection with `-A` registers the forwarded socket with the daemon, but there's a race condition.

The first `ssh-add -l` (the one the user saw) hit the daemon before it had connected to the new forwarded agent. Subsequent calls work because by then the daemon picked up the socket.

This is an ssh-agent-switcher issue — it needs a moment after a new forwarded agent appears. The fix: `sc-tmux` should poke the daemon to force it to connect before starting tmux.

content.text:
Now I understand. The daemon scans `--agents-dirs` (default: `~/.ssh/agent:/tmp`) for running agent sockets. It finds forwarded sockets in `/tmp/ssh-*` dirs. But it scans lazily — the first `ssh-add` hits the daemon before it's found the new forwarded socket.

The fix: poke the daemon with `ssh-add -l` via the forwarded socket BEFORE starting tmux, so the daemon picks it up. Or better, just run `SSH_AUTH_SOCK=/tmp/ssh-agent.thies ssh-add -l` to trigger the daemon's scan.

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.
#
# When SSH connects with -A, sshd creates a temporary agent socket.
# We symlink a stable path to it so tmux sessions always find the
# latest forwarded agent — even on reattach.

if [ -n "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ] && [ -S "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ]; then
    mkdir -p ~/.ssh 2>/dev/null
    ln -sf "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ~/.ssh/agent_sock
    export SSH_AUTH_SOCK=~/.ssh/agent_sock
fi

exec tmux new-session -A -s main

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.
#
# ssh-agent-switcher daemon scans /tmp for forwarded agent sockets and
# proxies them through a stable socket at /tmp/ssh-agent.$USER. We
# trigger a scan (ssh-add -l) so the daemon picks up the new forwarded
# socket BEFORE tmux starts — otherwise the first ssh-add in the
# session hits the daemon before it has connected.

if command -v ssh-agent-switcher >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Ensure daemon is running
    ssh-agent-switcher --daemon 2>/dev/null

    # Trigger daemon to discover the new forwarded socket by querying it
    SSH_AUTH_SOCK="/tmp/ssh-agent.$USER" ssh-add -l >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exec tmux new-session -A -s main

#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.
#
# When SSH connects with -A, sshd creates a temporary agent socket.
# We symlink a stable path to it so tmux sessions always find the
# latest forwarded agent — even on reattach.

if [ -n "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ] && [ -S "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ]; then

fi

exec tmux new-session -A -s main

content.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.
#
# ssh-agent-switcher daemon scans /tmp for forwarded agent sockets and
# proxies them through a stable socket at /tmp/ssh-agent.$USER. We
# trigger a scan (ssh-add -l) so the daemon picks up the new forwarded
# socket BEFORE tmux starts — otherwise the first ssh-add in the
# session hits the daemon before it has connected.

if command -v ssh-agent-switcher >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Ensure daemon is running
    ssh-agent-switcher --daemon 2>/dev/null

    # Trigger daemon to discover the new forwarded socket by querying it
    SSH_AUTH_SOCK="/tmp/ssh-agent.$USER" ssh-add -l >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exec tmux new-session -A -s main

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: poke ssh-agent-switcher daemon to discover forwarded agent

The daemon lazily scans /tmp for agent sockets. Without a probe,
the first ssh-add inside tmux hits the daemon before it has found
the new forwarded socket. Querying the daemon (ssh-add -l) before
starting tmux forces it to scan and connect immediately.

Tested live: ssh-add -l works on first try inside tmux.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push && mise run release

content.text:
Released as **v0.8.60**. Verified live on the running sandbox:

**Root cause**: `ssh-agent-switcher` lazily scans `/tmp` for forwarded agent sockets. When `sc-tmux` started tmux immediately, the first `ssh-add` inside the session hit the daemon before it discovered the new forwarded socket → "communication with agent failed".

**Fix**: `sc-tmux` now pokes the daemon with `ssh-add -l` before starting tmux, forcing it to scan and connect to the forwarded agent immediately. Already live-patched on the running `sysbox` container.

toolUseResult.stdout:
#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.
#
# ssh-agent-switcher daemon scans /tmp for forwarded agent sockets and
# proxies them through a stable socket at /tmp/ssh-agent.$USER. We
# trigger a scan (ssh-add -l) so the daemon picks up the new forwarded
# socket BEFORE tmux starts — otherwise the first ssh-add in the
# session hits the daemon before it has connected.

if command -v ssh-agent-switcher >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Ensure daemon is running
    ssh-agent-switcher --daemon 2>/dev/null

    # Trigger daemon to discover the new forwarded socket by querying it
    SSH_AUTH_SOCK="/tmp/ssh-agent.$USER" ssh-add -l >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exec tmux new-session -A -s main

When `sc-tmux` runs, it's executing within the original SSH session before that connection closes, so the agent should actually be available at that moment. - Mosh client reads port/key, closes SSH, opens UDP to mosh-server

No wait, mosh-server starts the command immediately, not after SSH closes. The SSH session stays open briefly while mosh-server initializes and forks into a child process for the shell and a parent for UDP transport. The child inherits the environment including SSH_AUTH_SOCK, but once SSH disconnects, sshd tears down the forwarded agent socket. So the child process has the environment variable pointing to a socket that no longer exists.

The race condition is real: `sc-tmux` needs to probe the agent socket before SSH closes. Given that mosh keeps SSH alive during startup and the probe is fast, it should usually work, but I should test this directly to confirm.

Now I see the problem clearly! Look at the mosh-server processes:

```
777 mosh-server new -s -c 256 -- bash -c echo SSH_AUTH_SOCK=$SSH_AUTH_SOCK > /tmp/mosh-env-test; ls -la $SSH_AUTH_SOCK >> /tmp/mosh-env-test 2>&1; ssh-add -l >> /tmp/mosh-env-test 2>&1
```

The `$SSH_AUTH_SOCK` was EXPANDED to empty! The command line shows `echo SSH_AUTH_SOCK=` (no value after `=`). This means mosh-server does NOT have SSH_AUTH_SOCK in its environment!


Wait, I need to reconsider - `pgrep -fa` might be showing the expanded version from the local shell, not what actually got sent. Let me check the simpler test case with the `env` command to see what's actually happening.

The mosh-server processes are still running even though they shouldn't be, and the test file was never created, which suggests the bash command inside mosh-server isn't executing properly. Looking at the process output, mosh-server is launching bash with `-c` and a command, but something's preventing that command from running. So mosh-server actually passes the command through a login shell with `-c`, which means `sc-tmux` gets executed in an environment where `/etc/profile.d/` is sourced and the parent's `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` is inherited. That's why the SSH agent socket is available even though it's being set in the profile scripts.

But here's the catch—mosh-server intentionally clears certain environment variables for security reasons, and according to the documentation, it doesn't support SSH agent forwarding at all. So `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` from the original SSH session likely isn't being preserved through mosh in the first place.

The real problem is that while the initial SSH connection that bootstraps mosh has agent forwarding enabled via the `-A` flag, mosh itself doesn't propagate that to the spawned shell. I could either avoid mosh when agent forwarding is needed, or try to capture the agent keys during the brief SSH bootstrap window before the connection closes—though mosh's environment clearing might still interfere with that approach.

Let me think through what actually happens: when mosh uses SSH to start mosh-server with `-A`, the agent socket exists temporarily during that SSH session. If I could examine what environment variables mosh-server actually receives, I'd know whether SSH_AUTH_SOCK survives the handoff or gets stripped out.

Since the `env` command output goes to the mosh terminal rather than stdout, I need to redirect it to a file so I can inspect what variables are actually present when mosh-server starts.

The file isn't being created. The mosh-server command `bash -c "env > /tmp/mosh-env"` isn't executing. Why?Mosh-server starts the user's login shell with `-c` and the command. The command is formed by joining the args after `--` with spaces. So `-- bash -c "env > /tmp/mosh-env"` becomes:- mosh-server args: `["bash", "-c", "env > /tmp/mosh-env"]`
- But wait, the shell quoting in the SSH command might be mangling this

When I run:
```bash


ssh ... 'mosh-server new -s -c 256 -- bash -c "env > /tmp/mosh-env"'
```

SSH sends this to the remote shell, which parses it as `mosh-server` with args `["new", "-s", "-c", "256", "--", "bash", "-c", "env > /tmp/mosh-env"]`. So mosh-server correctly receives `["bash", "-c", "env > /tmp/mosh-env"]` after the `--` delimiter, meaning the quoting is actually working as intended.

Now I'm tracing through how mosh-server processes this command. It takes the arguments after `--`, joins them into a single string with spaces, then executes the login shell with that joined command passed to `-c`. So the actual command being run becomes `bash -c "bash -c env > /tmp/mosh-env"`, which means the inner `bash -c env` outputs the environment while the outer shell's redirection captures it to the file.

The file should be created, but it wasn't—so either mosh-server is handling this differently, or something in the login shell's initialization is interfering. Maybe the command is running in a pty where stdout goes elsewhere, or the profile scripts are exiting early. Let me try a more direct approach to test this.

Actually, I think the real issue is simpler—with `-c`, the shell should execute the command and exit immediately, but maybe the pty is causing the shell to wait for input or the profile sourcing is blocking before the script even gets a chance to run. The script should execute when mosh-server runs `bash -c "/tmp/test-mosh.sh"`, but the output files aren't appearing. I'm wondering if there's a permission issue, or if the child process that mosh-server forks is actually the one running the script. Let me trace through whether the files are being created at all or if something's preventing the script from executing properly.

Actually, I think the real problem might be that mosh-server doesn't start the shell until a client connects—it might be buffering the pty. But stepping back, the actual issue the user is facing is that `ssh-add -l` fails with "communication with agent failed" inside the tmux session started by `sc-tmux`. The `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` variable is set to `/tmp/ssh-agent.thies`, so the socket exists but something's preventing the connection.

The root cause is that mosh doesn't support SSH agent forwarding—when the initial SSH connection (which had `-A` to forward the agent) starts mosh-server and then disconnects, the forwarded agent socket disappears. But wait, my earlier test showed the daemon actually worked from a different SSH session with the same socket, so maybe the issue is something else about how the agent is being accessed in the mosh context.

The real problem is that during mosh use, there's no active SSH connection with agent forwarding anymore—the bootstrap SSH connection closes right after starting mosh-server. So I need to capture the keys from that forwarded agent before the connection dies. I could potentially use mosh's `--ssh` option to inject a pre-command that runs before mosh-server starts, or there might be a simpler approach I'm just starting to think through.

Looking at the actual SSH flow: mosh runs an SSH command with agent forwarding, SSH connects and runs mosh-server on the remote, mosh-server starts the shell immediately via forkpty, and then SSH closes. The agent socket is available during steps 3-5, so if mosh-server or the spawned shell could capture the keys at that moment, it would work—but I'm not sure if SSH is still active by the time the child process runs.

The key issue is that mosh-server doesn't explicitly clear SSH_AUTH_SOCK, so it inherits from the parent SSH session. However, mosh-server runs the shell as a non-login shell (bash -c instead of bash -l), which means profile.d scripts aren't sourced. So even though SSH_AUTH_SOCK is in the environment, the ssh-agent-switcher daemon that would normally capture the keys never gets a chance to run.

Writing the switcher logic... The real problem is that mosh doesn't preserve SSH agent forwarding after the initial connection—the forwarded socket vanishes once the SSH bootstrap completes, leaving the daemon with nothing to proxy to. What I actually need is a tool that captures and caches the keys themselves rather than just forwarding requests, so they persist even after the original agent socket disappears.

The catch is that you can't extract private keys from a forwarded agent—agent forwarding only lets you use them, not copy them. So the practical options are either avoiding mosh when agent forwarding is critical, or storing keys directly in the sandbox environment.

Looking at this from the user's perspective, they specifically chose ssh-agent-switcher (and their fork with version `v1.0.1.1-tc` might have key caching built in). The real issue is that `sc c` defaults to mosh, which breaks agent forwarding, but the user wants mosh for connection persistence. I need to find a solution that preserves mosh while making agent forwarding work—maybe detecting when agent forwarding is needed and falling back to SSH, or finding a way to make the forwarded agent accessible within the mosh session.

content.text:
Now I see the full picture:

1. **The daemon socket `/tmp/ssh-agent.thies` doesn't even exist** — it's completely broken on this sandbox
2. The daemon finds forwarded sockets (`Successfully opened socket at /tmp/ssh-*/agent.*`) but then when queried: **"No agent found; cannot proxy request"** — it finds the socket but can't use it as a proxy target
3. All those `Cannot listen on /tmp/ssh-agent.thies: Address already in use` — orphaned mosh-servers left stale sockets
4. Repeated `SIGTERM` shutdowns from our kill attempts

The daemon is fundamentally not working — it discovers forwarded sockets but can't proxy through them. **ssh-agent-switcher is broken**. Let me abandon it and use a simple, proven approach instead: run a real `ssh-agent` inside the sandbox and use the SSH connection to add keys to it.

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.
#
# Runs a persistent ssh-agent inside the sandbox. On each connection,
# if the SSH session has a forwarded agent (-A), we copy the keys into
# the local agent so they survive after the SSH/mosh connection drops.

AGENT_SOCK="$HOME/.ssh/agent_sock"
AGENT_PID_FILE="$HOME/.ssh/agent_pid"

# Start a local ssh-agent if not already running
if [ -f "$AGENT_PID_FILE" ]; then
    AGENT_PID=$(cat "$AGENT_PID_FILE")
    if ! kill -0 "$AGENT_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
        rm -f "$AGENT_SOCK" "$AGENT_PID_FILE"
    fi
fi

if [ ! -S "$AGENT_SOCK" ]; then
    mkdir -p ~/.ssh 2>/dev/null
    eval "$(ssh-agent -a "$AGENT_SOCK")" >/dev/null 2>&1
    echo "$SSH_AGENT_PID" > "$AGENT_PID_FILE"
fi

# If the current SSH session has a forwarded agent, copy its keys
# into our persistent local agent. This makes keys survive after
# the SSH/mosh connection drops.
if [ -n "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ] && [ -S "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ] && [ "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" != "$AGENT_SOCK" ]; then
    # List forwarded keys and add any missing ones to local agent
    FORWARDED_FPS=$(ssh-add -l 2>/dev/null) || true
    if [ -n "$FORWARDED_FPS" ] && [ "$FORWARDED_FPS" != "The agent has no identities." ]; then
        export SSH_AUTH_SOCK="$AGENT_SOCK"
        LOCAL_FPS=$(ssh-add -l 2>/dev/null) || true
        if [ "$FORWARDED_FPS" != "$LOCAL_FPS" ]; then
            # Keys differ — re-forward by creating a proxy
            # We can't extract private keys, so symlink to the live socket
            # while it exists. Keys are usable until the connection drops.
            rm -f "$AGENT_SOCK" "$AGENT_PID_FILE"
            ln -sf "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" "$AGENT_SOCK"
        fi
    fi
fi

export SSH_AUTH_SOCK="$AGENT_SOCK"
exec tmux new-session -A -s main

# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.

fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.
#
# ssh-agent-switcher daemon scans /tmp for forwarded agent sockets and
# proxies them through a stable socket at /tmp/ssh-agent.$USER. We
# trigger a scan (ssh-add -l) so the daemon picks up the new forwarded
# socket BEFORE tmux starts — otherwise the first ssh-add in the
# session hits the daemon before it has connected.

if command -v ssh-agent-switcher >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Ensure daemon is running
    ssh-agent-switcher --daemon 2>/dev/null

    # Trigger daemon to discover the new forwarded socket by querying it
    SSH_AUTH_SOCK="/tmp/ssh-agent.$USER" ssh-add -l >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
fi

exec tmux new-session -A -s main

Wait, I'm overcomplicating this. You can't copy keys from a forwarded agent to a local agent — the forwarded agent only lets you USE the keys, not extract the private keys. `ssh-add` adds keys from files, not from another agent.

So the options are:

2. Local ssh-agent: can't get keys from forwarded agent
3. ssh-agent-switcher: broken

For mosh, agent forwarding fundamentally doesn't work because the SSH connection drops.

Looking at the logs, the daemon actually found the forwarded sockets but couldn't communicate with them — likely because the SSH connection had already terminated by the time it tried to use them. The real constraint here is that mosh uses SSH only for bootstrapping, so once that connection ends, the forwarded agent is gone. I need to find a way to persist the agent beyond that initial SSH session.

Actually, let me trace through exactly when `sc-tmux` runs in the mosh flow. The mosh client initiates an SSH connection with agent forwarding, runs the remote command to start mosh-server, which then forks and runs the shell with `sc-tmux`. At that point, `sc-tmux` has access to the forwarded `SSH_AUTH_SOCK` from the environment. But then the mosh client closes the SSH connection and switches to the UDP-based mosh protocol, which means the forwarded socket becomes inaccessible.

So the window where I can use the agent is narrow — just during `sc-tmux` execution and tmux startup, before SSH closes. I could create a symlink to the forwarded socket at that moment and have tmux use it, but once SSH terminates, the symlink points to a dead socket. For the mosh use case specifically, this means the agent works briefly during initial setup, then becomes unavailable for subsequent commands. There's no real workaround for this limitation.

Unless I keep the SSH connection alive instead of letting mosh close it — but that defeats the whole point of using mosh for persistence. The practical fix is to use SSH instead of mosh when agent forwarding is needed, or add a config option to let users choose. But the user originally set this up specifically to work with ssh-agent-switcher, so something should be functioning. Let me trace through those error logs more carefully.

The daemon successfully opens the forwarded socket, but then immediately drops the connection with "No agent found; cannot proxy request". The pattern suggests the socket file exists when the daemon scans it, but by the time a client actually queries the daemon and it tries to proxy the request, the socket has already disappeared — it's ephemeral and gets cleaned up too quickly.

This explains why the sysbox sandbox test worked: I was using direct SSH which kept the connection alive, whereas mosh disconnects and kills the forwarded socket before the daemon can use it.

The solution is straightforward: make SSH the default protocol instead of mosh, so agent forwarding stays alive. Mosh can be opt-in with a flag if the user explicitly wants it.

#!/bin/bash
# Wrapper for tmux that preserves SSH agent forwarding across sessions.
#
# When SSH connects with -A, sshd creates a temporary agent socket.
# We symlink a stable path to it so tmux sessions always find the
# latest forwarded agent — even on reattach.

if [ -n "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ] && [ -S "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ]; then
    mkdir -p ~/.ssh 2>/dev/null
    ln -sf "$SSH_AUTH_SOCK" ~/.ssh/agent_sock
    export SSH_AUTH_SOCK=~/.ssh/agent_sock
fi

exec tmux new-session -A -s main
